rationale: The citing paper uses Hoban and Bucklin (2015) as a key prior study to motivate its core heterogeneity logic, explicitly noting that ad responsiveness varies across purchase-funnel stages and adopting an online-recency segmentation approach "in line with" that work. It also engages the cited paper in interpretation of results by comparing estimated banner-ad elasticities against Hoban and Bucklin's reported elasticity benchmark. These uses go beyond a passing mention and materially inform framing and empirical positioning.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Hoban and Bucklin's purchase-funnel display-ad response line by testing stage/recency-based banner effects in a predominantly offline retail context. It adds cross-channel and cross-campaign outcomes, including indirect effects via website visits and offline purchase incidence.
